description: Add fashion-related icons throughout the site (scissors, tape measure, sewing machine, thread, fabric, mannequin, needle, etc.) using Lucide React. Create a new 'Craftsmanship' section showcasing the tailoring process with fashion tool icons. Enhance the Services section with richer fashion iconography. Add decorative fashion icons to section dividers and backgrounds.
steps: Create a new component src/components/Craftsmanship.tsx - a luxury 'Our Process' / 'The Atelier Experience' section that showcases Happiness's tailoring workflow with fashion icons. Structure: 4-5 steps shown as a horizontal or vertical timeline with large icons. Steps like: 1) Consultation (icon: MessageCircle or Palette), 2) Fabric Selection (icon: Scissors or SwatchBook), 3) Pattern Cutting (icon: Ruler + Scissors), 4) Hand Sewing (icon: a custom needle/thread icon or use Lucide's 'Needle' if available, else 'Pen' as sewing metaphor), 5) Final Fitting (icon: Shirt or User). Use Framer Motion scroll animations. Style it with the cream/bronze/wine palette and the .container-luxe pattern.
Add the Craftsmanship component to App.tsx - place it between About and Services sections for a natural flow: About (the designer) > Craftsmanship (the process) > Services (what she offers).

Enhance src/components/Services.tsx - add more fashion-specific Lucide icons. Replace generic icons with fashion-relevant ones where possible. Available Lucide icons to use: Scissors, Ruler, Shirt, Crown, Gem, Sparkles, Wand2 (already used), plus consider: Palette, Brush, Star, Layers, CircleDot (for bobbin/thread spool). Add a small decorative row of fashion tool icons (scissors, ruler, thread) as a visual divider above or below the services grid.

Add a decorative fashion icons strip to the Marquee component (src/components/Marquee.tsx). Intersperse small fashion tool icons (Scissors, Ruler, etc.) between the text items instead of just the star character. Import Scissors and Ruler from lucide-react and render them as small inline SVG icons between marquee items.

Add subtle floating/decorative fashion icons to the Hero section background (src/components/Hero.tsx). Add 3-4 small, semi-transparent fashion tool icons (scissors, thread spool, tape measure, needle) positioned absolutely with the float animation, similar to the existing decorative blur circles. Use low opacity (10-20%) so they're atmospheric, not distracting.

Add a 'Tools of the Trade' mini visual element to the About section (src/components/About.tsx) - a small row of fashion tool icons (Scissors, Ruler, thread icon, sewing machine icon) displayed subtly near the pillars section to reinforce the handcraft message.
acceptance_criteria: New Craftsmanship.tsx component exists and renders a timeline/process section with fashion icons
The component appears in App.tsx between About and Services
At least 5 different fashion-related Lucide icons are used across the site (Scissors, Ruler, Shirt, Crown, Gem already exist - add more like Palette, Layers, PenTool, or custom SVG needle/thread)
Marquee uses inline icons instead of plain star characters
Hero has subtle decorative fashion icons in the background
npx tsc -b passes with zero errors
findings: All changes compiled and built successfully. Used Scissors, Ruler, Pen, Palette, Layers, Shirt as the additional fashion icons across components. The Marquee now uses a MarqueeItem sub-component pattern with Scissors icons as separators. The animate-float CSS class was already present in the project for the Hero floating icons.
